Plaintiff Joseph Baribeau'S Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiffs' Inspection Demand, Set One
Off Calendar Jun-25-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Elsa Berry, Esq. of Akay Law, 415.764.1999, appearing for the plaintiff and moving party Joseph Baribeau;
Paige N. Pembrook, Esq. of Freeman Mathis & Gary LLP, 415.352.6447, appearing for defendant Brown's Company;
Salayha Ghoury, Esq. of Phillips Spalllas & Angstadt LLP, 415.278.9400 ext. 114, appearing by CourtCall for defendant Harry C. Thomas.

All parties set forth above stipulate to THIS MOTION being heard by Judge Pro Tem Melinda Derish.

Matter called. Plaintiff Joseph Baribeau'S Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiffs' Inspection Demand, Set One

The tentative ruling hearing was held solely on the issue of sanctions.

A R G U M E N T held. Ruling - At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling and awards sanctions against Plaintiff as follows: Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Defendants Laura Rogers and Brown & Company Real Estate, Inc.'s Responses to Plaintiff's Inspection Demand, Set One is denied. Plaintiff does not dispute he received defendants' responses and signed verifications via electronic mail on April 16, 2018, but argues he did not agree to accept electronic service and therefore the responses and verifications are invalid and defendants have waived objections. This argument is not persuasive in light of the submitted March 2018 email wherein defense counsel states that responses will be sent "via email" and plaintiff's counsel states "no problem." As to the issue of the validity of signed verifications provided by email, plaintiff's reliance on Thomas v. Makita, U.S.A., Inc. (1986) 181 Cal.App.3d 989 is misplaced. The Makita court did not address the issue of verifications provided by email, but rather the situation where one party's attorney lost the verification and therefore did not provide it to the opposing party. (Id. at 992-993.) Santions are awarded against plaintiff in the amount of $1,000.00. Order signed in open court. (See order filed this date for full terms.)

Judge Pro Tem: Melinda Derish, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. (302/JPT/HEK)

Notice Of Motion For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Jan-11-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DOUGLAS N. AKAY OF THE AKAY LAW, TEL. NO. 1-415-764-1999 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CHRISTOPHER M. MCDONALD OF PHILLIPS SPALLAS & ANGSTADT LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-278-9400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court vacated its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: PLAINTIFF JOSEPH BARIBEAU'S Motion For Attorneys Fees and costs pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Section 425.16 is Granted. Mr. Baribeau is the prevailing party on his anti-SLAPP motion to strike and his request for fees and costs is excessive and not sufficiently supported. The court awarded $15,000 in atty's fee. The reasonable loadstar for fees would be 30 hours of work for $450.00 an hour and 10 hours of paralegal work of $150.00 an hour for a total of $15,000. Mr. Baribeau's request for over $70,000 is not reasonable for the relatively simple issues on which he did prevail: the striking of the allegations relating to the complaints made to the Department of Building Inspection and the Police Department. Complaints made to a government agency are almost always protected activity and there are numerous authorities so holding and thus it should not have taken more than a few hours of legal work to prepare an anti-SLAPP motion seeking to strike such allegations. Nor were the merits issues relating to the complaints to the Department of Building Inspection and the Police Department either difficult or novel and thus they required minimal legal work as well. The absence of any time records to support the large amount of fees requested makes it impossible for the court and counsel for Mr. Thomas to properly evaluate the fees request. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk

Notice Of Special Motion To Strike And Special Motion To Strike The Pleadings
Off Calendar Nov-16-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Douglas Akay of Akay Law telephone number 415-764-1999 for Plaintiff Joseph Baribeau.
Christopher McDonald of Phillips Spallas & Angstadt LLP telephone number 415-278-9400 for Defendant Harry Thomas.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court Confirmed its tentative ruling.
Cross-defendant Joseph Baribeau's anti-SLAPP motion to strike is granted as to the portions of paragraph 13 of the cross-complaint filed by cross-complainant Harry Thomas that Mr. Baribeau made complaints to the Department of Building Inspection and the Police Department and denied in all other respects. While the portions of all three cross-claims that are based on the allegations that Mr. Baribeau made complaints to the Department of Building Inspection and the Police Department are stricken, all three cross-claims remain viable because, after removing the stricken allegations, each of them arise from non-incidental allegations of non-protected activity by Mr. Baribeau (i.e., trespassing and breach of contract terms). At the hearing on October 25, 2016 the court struck the allegations regarding Mr. Baribeau's complaints to the Department of Building Inspection and there is no basis to revisit that ruling. The allegations of complaints to the Police Department lack the required minimal merit to support any of the three cross-claims. There is no showing by Mr. Thomas that the statements allegedly made by Mr. Baribeau to the Police Department constitute a nuisance, breach of contract or negligent conduct. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Kelley Anne DeYoung, CSR #6780. (302/HK)
